title: amygdalus amygdalus
intro: Amygdalus amygdalus, also known as the bitter almond tree or wild bitter almond, is a small deciduous tree native to the Mediterranean region, producing edible nuts that resemble almonds but contain a toxic compound called amygdalin. The medicinal properties of Amygdalus amygdalus lie in its ability to stimulate the digestive system, relieve respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant. The health benefits of consuming Amygdalus amygdalus in moderation include relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues, as well as support for the immune system. Herbal preparations of Amygdalus amygdalus include teas, tinctures, and extracts that can be used to make cough syrups, expectorants, and digestive tonics.
